auth.name: Name
auth.brandFeature1: Build knowledge graphs from your data
auth.brandDescription: Enterprise multi-agent simulation platform. Model complex scenarios, analyze stakeholder dynamics, and make data-driven decisions.

home.title: Projects
home.subtitle: Manage your simulation projects
home.noProjectsHint: Create your first simulation project to get started

scenarios.fin_sentiment_doc_guide: Upload market research reports, financial news articles, analyst reports, earnings transcripts, or industry trend analysis. The system extracts companies, sectors, financial metrics, and market relationships.
scenarios.fin_sentiment_doc_examples: Examples: quarterly earnings reports, sector analysis papers, crypto market reports, central bank policy statements
scenarios.fin_sentiment_seed_guide: Describe the market scenario: what event or trend is happening, which sectors/companies are affected, what investor profiles should react. Include specific data points if available.
scenarios.fin_sentiment_seed_example: Example: "Bitcoin ETF approval has triggered a 40% rally. Traditional finance analysts are divided — some see crypto legitimizing, others warn of a bubble. Simulate reactions from retail investors, institutional traders, crypto enthusiasts, and risk-averse pension funds."
scenarios.content_lab_doc_guide: Upload content policies, community guidelines, social media posts, viral content examples, or platform moderation reports. The system maps content types, user behaviors, and moderation rules.
scenarios.content_lab_doc_examples: Examples: platform community guidelines, content moderation case studies, viral post datasets, misinformation reports
scenarios.content_lab_seed_guide: Describe the content scenario: what type of content is spreading, on which platform, what moderation challenges exist. Define user personas (creators, moderators, casual users).
scenarios.content_lab_seed_example: Example: "A deepfake video of a celebrity endorsing a scam product is going viral on the platform. Simulate how content creators, fact-checkers, casual users, and automated moderation systems respond over 48 hours."
scenarios.crisis_pr_doc_guide: Upload crisis communication plans, PR case studies, media coverage, stakeholder analysis, or brand reputation reports. The system extracts stakeholders, communication channels, and crisis response strategies.
scenarios.crisis_pr_doc_examples: Examples: corporate crisis case studies, media response templates, stakeholder mapping documents, social media monitoring reports
scenarios.crisis_pr_seed_guide: Describe the crisis: what happened, who is affected, what is the public reaction. Define stakeholders (media, customers, employees, regulators) and their initial positions.
scenarios.crisis_pr_seed_example: Example: "A major data breach exposed 2 million customer records. The company delayed disclosure by 3 weeks. Simulate media reactions, customer sentiment, regulatory response, and the effectiveness of different PR strategies."
scenarios.policy_lab_doc_guide: Upload policy papers, research reports, stakeholder position papers, economic impact assessments, or legislative proposals. The system maps stakeholders, policy options, economic data, and political positions.
scenarios.policy_lab_doc_examples: Examples: government white papers, think tank reports, industry position papers, economic impact assessments, public opinion surveys
scenarios.policy_lab_seed_guide: Describe the policy scenario: what policy is being proposed, who are the key stakeholders, what are their positions. Include specific numbers (costs, affected populations) and competing interests.
scenarios.policy_lab_seed_example: Example: "Hong Kong is evaluating AI workforce displacement policies affecting 60,000-160,000 jobs by 2030. Stakeholders: business lobby (oppose regulation), labor unions (demand safety nets), education sector (want reskilling funding), tech industry (prefer self-regulation). Simulate coalition dynamics and public opinion shifts across four policy options: AI tax, reskilling subsidies, portable benefits, and transition notices."
scenarios.war_game_doc_guide: Upload competitive intelligence reports, market analysis, company profiles, strategy documents, or industry benchmarks. The system maps competitors, market positions, strategic assets, and competitive dynamics.
scenarios.war_game_doc_examples: Examples: competitor analysis reports, market share data, SWOT analyses, industry trend reports, M&A case studies
scenarios.war_game_seed_guide: Describe the competitive scenario: which companies/players are involved, what market they compete in, what strategic moves are being considered. Define each player's strengths, weaknesses, and likely strategies.
scenarios.war_game_seed_example: Example: "Three ride-hailing companies compete in Southeast Asia. Company A has market dominance but high burn rate. Company B has strong tech but is new to the market. Company C has local partnerships but weak platform. Simulate their strategic moves over 12 months as a new regulatory framework is introduced."
scenarios.train_lab_doc_guide: Upload training scenarios, evaluation rubrics, behavioral guidelines, or reference materials. The system builds a knowledge base for agents to learn from during interactive training.
scenarios.train_lab_doc_examples: Examples: training manuals, scenario scripts, evaluation criteria, behavioral guidelines
scenarios.train_lab_seed_guide: Describe the training scenario: what skill or behavior you want to train/evaluate, what environment the agents operate in, and what success looks like.
scenarios.train_lab_seed_example: Example: "Train customer service agents to handle angry customers requesting refunds for a delayed product shipment. Agents should de-escalate, offer solutions, and maintain brand guidelines. Evaluate empathy, resolution time, and customer satisfaction."

step1.title: Step 1: Knowledge Graph
step1.subtitle: Upload seed documents and build the knowledge graph.
step1.guidanceGeneral: Upload documents that provide background context for your simulation. The system extracts entities, relationships, and key facts to build a knowledge graph that informs agent behavior.
step1.guidanceFormats: Supported formats: PDF, TXT, Markdown. Multiple uploads supported.

step2.title: Step 2: Environment Setup
step2.subtitle: Configure agents and simulation parameters.
step2.agentCountTip: Number of AI agents to participate. More agents = richer dynamics but longer runtime. Recommended: 5-15 for quick tests, 20-50 for detailed analysis.
step2.tickCountTip: Number of simulation rounds. Each tick represents one interaction cycle. Recommended: 10-20 for quick tests, 30-100 for deeper analysis.
step2.seedContextTip: The seed context sets the stage for the simulation. It tells agents who they are, what situation they're in, and what to discuss or react to.
step2.platformTip: Choose the social media platform to simulate. Twitter: short-form posts, hashtags, retweets. Reddit: threaded discussions, subreddits, upvotes.
step2.engineInfo: Engine selected based on scenario type
step2.createFailed: Failed to create simulation. Please try again.
step2.guidanceTitle: How to write a good seed context
step2.guidanceGeneral: A good seed context includes: the scenario setting, key stakeholders or groups, specific issues or events to react to, and what you want to observe.

step3.title: Step 3: Simulation
step3.subtitle: Run and monitor the simulation in real-time.
step3.noSimulation: No simulation configured. Please go back to Step 2 to create a simulation.

step4.title: Step 4: Report
step4.subtitle: View the generated analysis report.
step4.noSimulation: No simulation found. Please complete Step 3 first.

step5.title: Step 5: Deep Interaction
step5.subtitle: Chat with agents and explore insights.
step5.noSimulation: No simulation found. Please complete previous steps first.
step5.startChatting: Select an agent and start chatting

trainLab.subtitle: Interactive training simulation with manual actions.
